deeply.ex: I am deeply moved by your kindness.

deepness.def: 深、深度、深奧人物 (單數) (深)
deepness.ex: The_extreme deepness of sea is key.

deep-seated.def: 深層的、根深蒂固的人的人物 (根)
deep-seated.ex: She has a very deep-seated fear of dog.

deer.def: 鹿人物 (單數/複數同形) (鹿)
deer.ex: A herd of deer ran through the_woods.

deerstalker.def: 獵鹿帽、獵鹿者人物 (單數) (帽)

deface.def: 損傷外觀、汙損的人的人
deface.ex: It's a crime to deface local walls.

defacement.def: 汙損、損傷的人物 (單數) (損)

de-facto.def: 事實上的、實際上的人的人物 (實)
de-facto.ex: He is the_de-facto leader of the_firm.

defalcate.def: 盜用、挪用(公款)的人的人
defalcate.ex: He tried to defalcate public money.

defalcation.ex: A large defalcation was found now.

defamatory.def: 誹謗的、中傷的人的人物 (單數) (誹)

defame.ex: They tried to defame the_mayor now.

default.def: 違約、不履行、預設、拖欠的人的人人物 (單數) (違/預)
default.col: by default, default setting, win by default
default.ex: The_firm is likely to default on loan.

defaulter.def: 違約者、拖欠者人物 (單數) (違)

defeat.def: 擊敗、失敗、挫折、擊敗者的人的人人物 (單數) (敗)

defeated.def: 被擊敗的、垂頭喪氣的人的人 (或物) (敗)
defeated.ex: He looked defeated and very tired.

defeatism.ex: We must fight against defeatism now.

defeatist.def: 失敗主義者、消極的人的人物 (單數) (消)
defeatist.ex: He has a very defeatist attitude today.

defecation.ex: Regular defecation is key to health.

defect.def: 缺陷、缺點、背叛、叛逃的人的人人物 (單數) (缺/叛)
defect.ex: The_car has a serious technical defect.

defective.def: 有缺陷的、不完備的人的人物 (單數) (缺)

defector.def: 叛逃者、逃兵人物 (單數) (叛)

defence/defense.def: 防禦、辯護、防務人物 (單數) (防/辯)
defence/defense.col: self defence, ministry of defence, in defence of, criminal defence
defence/defense.ex: What was his common local defence?

defend.def: 防守、辯護、保護的人的人
defend.col: defend title, defend oneself, defend from
defend.ex: I'm ready to defend my local honor.

defender.def: 防禦者、擁護者、公設辯護人人物 (單數) (防)
defender.ex: He is a great defender of free speech.

defensible.def: 可防禦的、正當的人的人物 (單數) (防)

defensive.def: 防禦性的、自衛的人人物 (單數) (防)
defensive.ex: He put me on the_defensive today.

defer.def: 推遲、延期、順從、聽從的人的人

deference.def: 順從、敬意、尊重人物 (單數) (敬)
deference.col: out of deference to, show deference
deference.ex: I spoke in low tone out of deference.
